hint: Focus on ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls. Key areas: policies (A.5.1), roles (A.5.2), access control (A.5.15, A.8.2, A.8.3), authentication (A.8.5), network security (A.8.20), and termination responsibilities (A.6.5).

sections.heading: Purpose and Scope
sections.text_summary: Establishes the governance framework, management direction, accountability, and scope of the access control policy.

grader_notes.A.5.1: Policy framework and management direction for information security is described.
grader_notes.A.5.2: Roles and responsibilities, accountability across personnel types defined.

sections.heading: User Access Management
sections.text_summary: Formal access request process, need-to-know/least privilege provisioning, quarterly review, and RBAC enforcement.

grader_notes.A.5.15: Access control policy with authorisation rules and need-to-know basis.
grader_notes.A.8.3: Information access restriction via RBAC and role profiles.

sections.heading: Privileged Access
sections.text_summary: PAM registry, separate admin accounts, JIT access, session logging, and monitoring of all privileged activity.

grader_notes.A.8.2: Privileged access rights management: least privilege, PAM, separate accounts, monitoring.

sections.heading: Remote Access
sections.text_summary: VPN requirement, session timeout after 15 minutes inactivity, device compliance, network encryption.

grader_notes.A.8.5: Secure authentication for remote sessions with inactivity timeout (re-authentication).
grader_notes.A.8.20: Network security: VPN, firewall rules, encrypted channels for remote connections.

sections.heading: Access Review and Termination
sections.text_summary: Bi-annual access reviews, 24-hour revocation on termination, rights cleared on role change.

grader_notes.A.5.15: Access rights management — review and revocation processes.
grader_notes.A.6.5: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment: immediate revocation.
